steps.title: Read two points from the table
steps.content: From the table: when $$c=0$$ cars, the max passengers and crew is $$p=9$$. When $$c=1$$, $$p=64$$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$55c-p=-9$': Equivalent to $$p=55c+9$$. Gives $$p=9$$ when $$c=0$$ and $$p=64$$ when $$c=1$$. ✓
  • Choice B — '$55c-p=9$': Equivalent to $$p=55c-9$$. Gives $$p=-9$$ when $$c=0$$ — negative passengers is impossible. ✗
  • Choice C — '$55p-c=-9$': Has the wrong variable as the coefficient of 55. When $$c=0$$: $$p=-9/55$$, not an integer. ✗
  • Choice D — '$55p-c=9$': Same issue — 55 multiplies $$p$$ instead of $$c$$. Gives non-integer $$p$$ values. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
